facts.text: Hartsdale Train Station, built in 1896, is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and was once the town’s post office.
The town hosts America’s first canine pet cemetery, founded by veterinarian Samuel Johnson in 1896, and it remains the oldest continuously operated pet cemetery worldwide.
Hartsdale was the birthplace of Carvel ice cream; the first Carvel store opened in 1934 and the town celebrated its invention with “Flying Saucers” after Memorial Day parades.
During the Revolutionary War, the Battle of White Plains unfolded along the Bronx River near the train station site, linking Hartsdale to a pivotal moment in American history.
The original Hartsdale railroad depot served as the town’s post office in 1870, with the station agent acting as postmaster until the depot burned in 1898.
The Greenburgh Public Library’s Hartsdale Free Library operated from the second floor between 1914 and 1919, reflecting the town’s early commitment to public learning.
